text.title: django-imports Decision Tree
text.summary: Per cross-app dependency: which import strategy?

decision-tree.root-question: In what context does this cross-app symbol get used?

branch.conclusion: Use string reference: ForeignKey("users.User", ...). No module-level import needed.
Use apps.get_model("users", "User") inside the function body. Avoids module-level circular import.
Import inside `if TYPE_CHECKING:` block. Pair with `from __future__ import annotations` UNLESS the module is a DRF serializer.

branch.question: Are 3+ apps cyclically depending on this symbol?

branch.conclusion: Extract the shared type / base class to core/. core/ imports from no other app.
Import at module level with mandatory alias: `from apps.users import models as user_models`. Reference as `user_models.User`.

text.example: context: Billing.Invoice has FK to accounts.User q-usage-context -> 'model-field' => conclusion r6-string-fk: ForeignKey("accounts.User", on_delete=PROTECT).
